what_it_does: Serves as a descriptive terrain index for the orienteering/ region, presenting relation-grouped tables that record each mapped item's kind, status, size, last-read date, and filesystem path, plus a Dataview live-query stub.
observations: Frontmatter declares type terrain-index, register descriptive, status unconfirmed, and coverage over all files read on 2026-06-26.
The body contains six markdown tables grouped by relation: company, external, connected, candidate, resource.
It states the tables are generated from entry frontmatter and should not be hand-maintained.
A Dataview query block is included to render live data from orienteering/entries.
Every listed entry currently has status unconfirmed.
It explicitly frames itself as a sub-map linked from [[Company Map]], not a competing home.
standouts: Self-labels as descriptive, not a blueprint, and unconfirmed until Tim confirms a lifecycle.
The external relation table is large and includes very large engine/data paths such as voice-venvs (48G) and vllm-env (8.4G).
Static markdown tables coexist with a Dataview query that would render the same data dynamically.
All entries share the same unconfirmed status and identical last-read date.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with type, register, aliases, tags, status, coverage, relates-to, and part-of.
Groups entries by a relation taxonomy: company, external, connected, candidate, resource.
Uses a Dataview code block for a live query renderer.
References schema/rules in external constitution and vault convention notes.
concerns: The static markdown tables claim to be generated from frontmatter, so hand edits risk divergence from the source.
All statuses are unconfirmed, making the index a raw scan rather than a validated inventory.
The Dataview query references an orienteering/entries path whose contents are not shown here.
No regeneration command or timestamp is visible to prove the tables are current.
notes: A self-described descriptive map page that renders orienteering entries into relation tables, backed by a Dataview query and currently marked unconfirmed.
questions: How exactly are the static tables regenerated from entry frontmatter?
Does the orienteering/entries folder exist and do its notes match the rows shown?
What lifecycle confirmation by Tim changes an entry from unconfirmed, and to what statuses?
Is the Dataview query functional in the current rendering environment?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be a generated, descriptive region sub-map; the file actually presents static markdown tables plus a Dataview query, so it functions as both a rendered snapshot and a live-query stub.
apparent_themes: Terrain mapping / orienteering metaphor for the codebase and filesystem.
Single source of truth driven by entry frontmatter.
Lifecycle confirmation gate before entries become authoritative.
intention_signals: To provide a navigable, auto-refreshable inventory of the company's filesystem and project orbit, while explicitly avoiding competing authority with the vault home page.
novelty: Uses an orienteering/terrain metaphor and a Dataview-backed, frontmatter-driven index to map filesystem locations alongside semantic relations.
contribution: A relation-grouped, frontmatter-driven index template and an explicit descriptive, unconfirmed stance that separates observed terrain from approved lifecycle.
summary_for_embedding: A descriptive terrain index for the orienteering/ region, listing filesystem entries by relation with kind, status, size, last-read date, and path, and including a Dataview live-query stub.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single-source, auto-refreshable map of the company's project orbit without competing with the main vault home.
